untranslatable_reason: Signal requires domain-specific commodity metrics not available in standard OHLCV indicators: (1) inventory levels as percentage of historical range, (2) days-to-cover calculation (inventory/daily demand rate), (3) production schedule lead times (supply arrival dates), (4) storage cost index. These are fundamental commodity data points not derivable from price/volume alone. Best approximation used price_pos_20 for relative positioning and hvol_20 for volatility regime, but core logic cannot be accurately expressed without commodity-specific data feeds.
translation_notes: The signal fundamentally describes a commodity microstructure arbitrage based on supply-demand imbalances and carrying costs. Attempted mapping: price_pos_20 < 0.25 approximates 'inventory at 25th percentile' (price at bottom of range suggests tight supply), volume < 20 approximates 'days-to-cover < 20' (sparse turnover suggests low availability), hvol_20 > 0.75 approximates 'high storage cost burden' (elevated volatility). However, without actual inventory data, supply schedules, and storage cost indices, this is a weak proxy. Recommend implementing custom data connectors for commodity fundamentals.
